name: only_zero_aligned
code:
proof fn phi_1_only_zero_aligned(addr: nat, size: nat)
    requires
        size > 0,
        aligned(addr, size),
    ensures
        addr == 0nat,
{
}
reason: If provable, aligned would be trivially unsatisfiable for any nonzero address, making all page-table alignment constraints vacuous.

name: 4k_implies_8k_aligned
code:
proof fn phi_2_4k_implies_8k_aligned(addr: nat)
    requires
        aligned(addr, 4096),
    ensures
        aligned(addr, 8192),
{
}
reason: 4K-alignment must not entail 8K-alignment; if provable, the pml4 alignment invariant would silently impose a stronger-than-intended constraint.

name: unique_alignment_size
code:
proof fn phi_3_unique_alignment_size(addr: nat, s1: nat, s2: nat)
    requires
        s1 > 0,
        s2 > 0,
        s1 != s2,
        aligned(addr, s1),
        aligned(addr, s2),
    ensures
        false,
{
}
reason: An address can be aligned to multiple sizes (e.g., 0 is aligned to every size); if provable, aligned would be inconsistent.

name: self_alignment_false
code:
proof fn phi_4_self_alignment_false(size: nat)
    requires
        size > 0,
    ensures
        !aligned(size, size),
{
}
reason: Every nonzero n satisfies n % n == 0, so aligned(n,n) must be true; if refuted, the modulus definition is broken.

name: aligned_bounded_by_size
code:
proof fn phi_5_aligned_bounded_by_size(addr: nat)
    requires
        aligned(addr, 4096),
    ensures
        addr <= 4096nat,
{
}
reason: Aligned addresses are unbounded multiples of the alignment; if provable, any address beyond 4096 would be wrongly excluded from valid page-table bases.
